2604.21530.title: Attention-based multiple instance learning for predominant growth pattern prediction in lung adenocarcinoma wsi using foundation models
2604.21530.paper_summary: Lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) grading depends on accurately identifying growth patterns, which are indicators of prognosis and can influence treatment decisions. Common deep learning approaches to determine the predominant pattern rely on patch-level classification or segmentation, requiring extensive annotations. This study proposes an attention-based multiple instance learning (ABMIL) framework to predict the predominant LUAD growth pattern at the whole slide level to reduce annotation burden. Our approach integrates pretrained pathology foundation models as patch encoders, used either frozen or fine-tuned on annotated patches, to extract discriminative features that are aggregated through attention mechanisms. Experiments show that fine-tuned encoders improve performance, with Prov-GigaPath achieving the highest agreement (\k{appa} = 0.699) under ABMIL. Compared to simple patch-aggregation baselines, ABMIL yields more robust predictions by leveraging slide-level supervision and spatial attention. Future work will extend this framework to estimate the full distribution of growth patterns and validate performance on external cohorts.
2604.21530.paper_summary_zh: 肺腺癌 (LUAD) 的分級依賴於準確識別生長模式，這些模式是預後的指標並且可以影響治療決策。常見的深度學習方法用於確定主要模式依賴於補丁級別的分類或分割，這需要大量的標註。這項研究提出了一種基於注意力的多實例學習 (ABMIL) 框架，以在整個切片級別預測主要的 LUAD 生長模式，以減少標註負擔。我們的方法整合了預訓練的病理基礎模型作為補丁編碼器，這些編碼器可以在標註的補丁上凍結或微調，以提取通過注意力機制聚合的區分特徵。實驗顯示，微調的編碼器提高了性能，Prov-GigaPath 在 ABMIL 下達到最高的一致性 (\k{appa} = 0.699)。與簡單的補丁聚合基線相比，ABMIL 通過利用切片級別的監督和空間注意力產生了更穩健的預測。未來的工作將擴展此框架以估計生長模式的完整分佈並在外部隊列上驗證性能。
2604.21530.author: Laura Valeria Perez-Herrera et.al.
2604.21530.authors: Laura Valeria Perez-Herrera, M. J. Garcia-Gonzalez, Karen Lopez-Linares
